steps.title: Interpret the constraint
steps.content: The phrase no more than 12 dollars describes a limit on spending. In inequality notation, no more than means ≤. So the total cost must satisfy (total cost) ≤ 12.

steps.title: Identify the cost expression
steps.content: Each orange costs 0.67 dollars, and we're buying $x$ oranges, so the base cost is $0.67x$. The problem includes a 2-dollar adjustment (shown as a minus sign), making the total cost: $0.67x - 2$

steps.title: Set up and identify the inequality
steps.content:
Combining the cost expression with the constraint:
$$0.67x - 2 \le 12$$
That matches Choice C — $0.67x - 2 \le 12$.
